items.briefing:
Registered training organisations (RTOs) should take note of a growing trend where cybercriminals are shifting their focus from educational institutions directly to the software suppliers that serve them. Compromising one supplier can give attackers access to many organisations at once, so it's worth checking what third-party edtech platforms your RTO relies on and whether those vendors have strong security practices. Separately, a new report confirms that most ransomware victims only discover an attack *after* their data has already been stolen — meaning damage is often done before anyone notices.

**Practical tip:** Ask your key software suppliers what security measures they have in place — a reputable vendor should be able to answer clearly and confidently.

featured.title: ASD to retire Essential Eight within two years, consults on replacement
featured.quote: Australia's cybersecurity framework is being fundamentally replaced after years of reliance.
